---
label: "Applying Agent Patterns"
icon: "📱"
---

## From Theory to Product: The SnapSense Agent Layer

Weeks 1-3 taught you LangGraph, Agentic RAG, and Multi-Agent patterns. This week, you apply everything to a real product: SnapSense AI.

### The Current SnapSense Pipeline

<CodeBlock b64="" />

This is a linear pipeline — no self-correction, no quality evaluation, no dynamic routing. If ML Kit misclassifies an object (sabudana as "plant"), the entire downstream output is wrong.

### The Agent-Enhanced Pipeline

<CodeBlock b64="" />

<AnalogyBox emoji="🏭" title="MDM Data Pipeline vs AI Agent Pipeline">
The SnapSense agent layer mirrors an MDM data pipeline: ingest (camera scan) → classify (category routing) → validate (data quality grading) → enrich (API lookups) → quality gate (human review for edge cases) → publish (generate tiles). Every MDM pipeline principle applies here.
</AnalogyBox>

<Quiz
  question="SnapSense currently misclassifies TERRO ant bait as 'food' via the barcode path. Which agent layer component would fix this?"
  options={["Better camera hardware", "The Category Agent using LLM override of ML Kit classification", "A larger LLM model", "More training data for ML Kit"]}
  correctIndex={1}
  explanation="The Category Agent receives ML Kit's classification AND the LLM's assessment, then decides the final category. If ML Kit says 'food' but the LLM recognizes 'TERRO ant bait' as a pest control product, the LLM override corrects the category before routing. This is the exclusion engine fix."
/>

<SeeItInRe3 text="Re³'s Forge agent does exactly this: it evaluates the topic, classifies it, and routes to the right specialists — not blindly, but with quality-checked classification." />
